source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Route Changes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Route Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network route changes.
input_row.rationale: Google Cloud Platform (GCP) routes define the paths network traffic takes from a VM instance to another destination. The other destination can be inside the organization VPC network (such as another VM) or outside of it. Every route consists of a destination and a next hop. Traffic whose destination IP is within the destination range is sent to the next hop for delivery. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through an expected path. Impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure that the prescribed Log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure that at least one metric <Log_Metric_Name> is present with the filter text: resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert") Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name> stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than zero(0) seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alert thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notification channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert") 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed Log Metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert") 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed alert policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page displays. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value ensures that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notification channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed the alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/logs-based-metrics/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/custom-metrics/ 3. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerts/ 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 5. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam 6. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/logging/metrics/create 7. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/alpha/monitoring/policies/create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field in the control provides detailed Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands to check for the existence of the log metric and alert policy, indicating the presence of GCP APIs or CLI functionality to automate these checks.
- The aspect being audited involves GCP services such as logging and monitoring, which are standard GCP features that can be queried via APIs.
- The control involves verifying configuration settings, such as log metrics and alerting policies, which can be accessed programmatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_alerts_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to verify the existence of the specified log metric.
  - Command: `gcloud logging metrics list --format json`
  - Check for a metric with filter:
    ```
    resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert")
    ```
  - Validate that the `metricDescriptor.type` is in the format `logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log_Metric_Name>`.

- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to verify the presence of an alerting policy related to the log metric.
  - Command: `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json`
  - Check for an alert policy with:
    - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"`
    - `enabled` set to `true`
  
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: At least one log metric exists matching the specified filters and there is a corresponding alerting policy correctly configured and enabled.
  - Fail: If such metrics or alerting policies are missing or incorrectly configured. 

This automated audit approach leverages GCP CLI and APIs to ensure compliance with the CIS benchmark regarding monitoring and alerting of VPC route changes.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. Both the existence of log-based metrics and alerting policies in GCP can be fully validated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud` CLI). No manual inspection is required if the checks are implemented correctly.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  - **Log Metrics:** `gcloud logging metrics list` can enumerate all log-based metrics, and the output can be filtered for the required filter string.
  - **Alerting Policies:** `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` (or the GA `gcloud monitoring policies list` in newer SDK versions) can list alerting policies, and their conditions can be parsed for the relevant metric.
- **CloudFormation / GCP Config:**  
  Not directly applicable for this check, as GCP Config does not natively support log metric/alert policy resources for compliance evaluation at this granularity.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** **Score: 8/10**

- **Strengths:**
  - The described `gcloud` commands are valid and appropriate for the resources in question.
  - The filter string for the log metric is correct for detecting VPC route changes.
  - The check for the alerting policy’s condition and enablement is accurate.

- **Minor Issues:**
  - **Program Name:** The program name `gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_alerts_configured` is descriptive but could be more precise (see Improvements).
  - **Alert Policy Command:** The use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` is outdated; `gcloud monitoring policies list` is now GA and should be preferred for production automation.
  - **Metric Name Matching:** The check for `metricDescriptor.type` should ensure it matches the user-defined metric name, but the logic for extracting and matching the metric name is not fully described.
  - **Alert Policy Condition:** The check for `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is correct, but the JSON structure can be complex; robust parsing is needed.
  - **Multiple Projects:** The approach assumes a single project context. In multi-project environments, this should be parameterized.

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:**  
  Suggest: `gcp_logging_vpc_route_change_alert_policy_configured`  
  (Singular "route_change" is more precise; "alert_policy" instead of "alerts" clarifies the resource type.)

- **Command Updates:**  
  - Use `gcloud monitoring policies list` instead of the `alpha` command for stability.
  - Consider adding `--project` parameterization for multi-project environments.

- **Metric Filter Robustness:**  
  - The metric filter should be matched exactly, not just substring, to avoid false positives.
  - Consider validating the metric’s filter logic, not just its existence.

- **Alert Policy Validation:**  
  - Ensure that the alert policy is not only enabled but also triggers on the correct metric and has appropriate notification channels.
  - Optionally, check that the alerting policy is not in a "muted" or "disabled" state.

- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - For more robust automation, consider using the [Cloud Monitoring API](https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/ref_v3/rest/v3/projects.alertPolicies/list) and [Logging API](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/v2/rest/v2/projects.metrics/list) directly for large-scale or cross-project checks.

- **Documentation:**  
  - Reference the CIS Benchmark section and provide example output for both pass and fail cases.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is technically sound and leverages standard, well-supported GCP CLI/API methods.
- Minor improvements are mostly about robustness and clarity, not fundamental correctness.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** MODIFY

- **Keep the automated approach, but:**
  - Update the alert policy command to use the GA (`gcloud monitoring policies list`) version.
  - Refine the program name to `gcp_logging_vpc_route_change_alert_policy_configured`.
  - Clarify in documentation that the metric filter and alert policy matching should be exact and robust.
  - Optionally, add guidance for multi-project environments and for checking notification channels.

---

**Summary:**  
The junior analyst’s assessment is fundamentally correct and technically accurate for an automated audit. Minor updates to command usage, naming, and robustness of checks are recommended for production-grade compliance automation. No reclassification is needed.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the existence of log-based metrics and alerting policies for VPC route changes can be fully validated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The required properties—log metric filters and alerting policy conditions—are directly accessible via these APIs, making manual inspection unnecessary. The audit steps map cleanly to programmatic checks.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_logging_log_metric_and_alert_for_vpc_route_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:**  
  - Logging (Log-based Metrics)
  - Monitoring (Alerting Policies)
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format=json`
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list --format=json` (use `alpha` or GA as available)
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - Log Metric: Existence of a user-defined metric with filter  
    ```
    resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert")
    ```
  - Alerting Policy: Existence of an enabled alerting policy with a condition referencing the above log metric (i.e., `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log_Metric_Name>"`)
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - At least one log-based metric exists with the specified filter.
  - At least one enabled alerting policy exists with a condition monitoring the above metric.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - No log-based metric with the required filter exists, or
  - No enabled alerting policy is configured for the required metric.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported automation. The only minor critique was on program naming precision, which has been addressed by specifying a clear, descriptive function name.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
